articles.title: ElvalHalcor to raise €250m in share capital and invest €455m by 2030
articles.summary: ElvalHalcor, parent company of Greek flat-rolled aluminium producer Elval, announced a €250 million new share capital raise to fund a €455 million investment programme running through 2030, targeting production capacity expansion, aluminium recycling infrastructure, and manufacturing efficiency improvements.
articles.novelis_angle: A €455m capacity push by Elval — one of the largest European FRP producers — signals escalating competition in the packaging and automotive sheet segments where Novelis's Nachterstedt, Pieve Emanuele, and Bresso plants are primary incumbents.

articles.title: JLR holds annual investor day
articles.summary: At its Gaydon Investor Day on 17 June, JLR announced the Electrified Modular Architecture (EMA) platform underpinning a Range Rover Electric due H2 FY2026-27 (76,976 on its waitlist), Range Rover Sport Electric, electrified Defender and Discovery variants, and the Jaguar Type 01 as Jaguar's first full EV; JLR confirmed five new model launches over 18 months and £3.7 billion in investment by FY27.
articles.novelis_angle: JLR committing five aluminium-intensive EMA models over 18 months is a direct sustained-demand signal for Novelis, whose REALCAR closed-loop partnership supplies BIW sheet to JLR's Solihull and Halewood plants.

articles.title: Middle East outages to keep aluminium market tight near-term, but Indonesia-China supply wave caps upside: Goldman Sachs
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs raised its Q3 2026 LME aluminium price forecast to $3,300/t (from $3,200/t) and cut West Asian production estimates by 660,000 tonnes for 2026, citing continued damage to Gulf smelting infrastructure; the bank notes that rising output from Indonesian and Chinese smelters limits any sustained upward move above this level.

articles.title: Aluminum falls as peace talk progress points to returning supply
articles.summary: LME aluminium touched a three-month low on 23 June after the US issued a 60-day licence permitting Iran to sell oil on international markets, reinforcing expectations that the Strait of Hormuz will formally reopen and restore supply from a region accounting for roughly 9% of global aluminium production.
